```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Function to calculate Manhattan distance and direction
    def manhattan_distance_and_direction(x1, y1, x2, y2):
        dx = x1 - x2
        dy = y1 - y2
        distance = abs(dx) + abs(dy)
        
        direction = []
        # Prioritize N/S then E/W for primary direction, then combine
        if dy > 0:
            direction.append("north")
        elif dy < 0:
            direction.append("south")
        
        if dx > 0:
            direction.append("west")
        elif dx < 0:
            direction.append("east")

        if not direction and distance == 0:
            return 0, "at current location"
        elif not direction: # e.g., if dx=0 or dy=0 (straight N/S/E/W)
            if dy > 0: direction.append("north")
            elif dy < 0: direction.append("south")
            if dx > 0: direction.append("west")
            elif dx < 0: direction.append("east")

        return distance, " to the " + " ".join(direction) if direction else ""

    agent_x, agent_y = None, None # These will hold the *absolute* coordinates of the agent
    agent_character_class = None
    visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1 # Relative visual coordinates of '@' on the map section

    # --- Agent Coords and Class Extraction (Robust) ---
    # 1. Attempt to extract from cursor line with full details (Yourself a <class> (x,y))
    agent_full_cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*Yourself a (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman|troglodyte|digger|aspirant|rhizotomist)\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_full_cursor_match:
        agent_character_class = agent_full_cursor_match.group(1)
        agent_x = int(agent_full_cursor_match.group(2))
        agent_y = int(agent_full_cursor_match.group(3))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_map_coordinates: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}.")
    
    # 2. If coordinates are not found in full cursor, try to get them from simpler cursor line (x,y)
    if agent_x is None or agent_y is None:
        agent_coord_cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*\n\s*\(x=(\d+), y=(\d+)\)", observation_text)
        if agent_coord_cursor_match:
            agent_x = int(agent_coord_cursor_match.group(1))
            agent_y = int(agent_coord_cursor_match.group(2))
            extracted_info.append(f"Agent_map_coordinates: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")
    
    # 3. Always try to extract character class from the agent info line after map if not already set
    if agent_character_class is None:
        agent_class_info_match = re.search(r"Agent the (Digger|Aspirant|Rhizotomist|Troglodyte|archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman)\s+St:", observation_text)
        if agent_class_info_match:
            agent_character_class = agent_class_info_match.group(1)
            # Only append if we actually found a class and it wasn't already added from agent_full_cursor_match
            if agent_character_class and not any("Agent_character_class" in s for s in extracted_info):
                 extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}.")

    # --- Map Information Extraction ---
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
        map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant") # Added for different agent description
        map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Rhizotomist") # Added for different agent description
        map_end_marker_4 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Troglodyte") # Added for different agent description
        map_end_marker_5 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line
        map_end_marker_6 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nNo  Points") # Fallback for death screen

        end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3, map_end_marker_4, map_end_marker_5, map_end_marker_6] if marker != -1]
        
        map_end_offset = -1
        if end_markers:
            map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines (which now include original padding)
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            # Proceed with map feature extraction only if agent's absolute position is known
            # and if the agent symbol was found on the map.
            if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None and visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Calculate the offset between the absolute agent coordinates and its visual position on the map.
                current_map_offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                current_map_offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                # --- Comprehensive Map Feature Collection (all visible features) ---
                all_raw_map_features = [] # To store (abs_x, abs_y, char_on_map, feature_type_string) for EVERYTHING on map
                walls = []
                open_floor = []
                molten_lava = []
                fountains = []
                stairs_up = []
                stairs_down = []
                corridors = []
                monsters_rat = []
                dropped_items = []
                
                for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                        abs_x = x_offset + current_map_offset_x
                        abs_y = y_offset + current_map_offset_y

                        feature_type_str = "unknown" # Default type
                        if char == '|' or char == '-': # walls (horizontal or vertical)
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "wall"
                        elif char == '.':
                            open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "open floor"
                        elif char == '}': 
                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "molten lava"
                        elif char == 'F': 
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "fountain"
                        elif char == '<': 
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "stairs up"
                        elif char == '>': 
                            stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "stairs down"
                        elif char == ':': 
                            corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "corridor"
                        elif char == 'r': # Example: rat monster
                            monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type_str = "rat monster"
                        elif char == 'd': # Example: dropped item or door (need context)
                            dropped_items.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'dropped item')) # Assuming 'd' character here is a generic dropped item
                            feature_type_str = "dropped item" # Overwrite if it's confirmed as a door later

                        all_raw_map_features.append((abs_x, abs_y, char, feature_type_str)) # Store all detected features

                # --- Process for Adjacent Features and Movement Options ---
                safe_adjacent_move_targets = []
                lethal_adjacent_move_targets = []
                monsters_within_striking_distance = []
                immediate_move_options = {}
                adjacent_features_for_interaction = {}
                
                # Directions for adjacent moves for consistent ordering
                adjacent_directions = {
                    (0, -1): "north", (1, -1): "northeast", (1, 0): "east", (1, 1): "southeast",
                    (0, 1): "south", (-1, 1): "southwest", (-1, 0): "west", (-1, -1): "northwest"
                }
                ordered_directions = ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]


                for fx, fy, char_on_map, feature_type_str in all_raw_map_features:
                    is_adjacent_char = (abs(agent_x - fx) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - fy) <= 1 and (fx != agent_x or fy != agent_y))
                    
                    if is_adjacent_char:
                        dx = fx - agent_x
                        dy = fy - agent_y
                        direction_key = (dx, dy)
                        move_dir = adjacent_directions.get(direction_key) # This will always find a direction if is_adjacent_char is true
                        
                        if move_dir: # Should always be true here
                            if feature_type_str == "molten lava":
                                lethal_adjacent_move_targets.append((fx, fy))
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "LETHAL (molten lava)"
                            elif feature_type_str == "rat monster":
                                monsters_within_striking_distance.append((fx, fy, 'rat'))
                                immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "ATTACK (rat)"
                            elif feature_type_str == "wall":
                                # Walls are not traversable move targets, but are interaction targets
                                # No `immediate_move_options` entry for direct movement onto a wall
                                adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir] = (fx, fy, feature_type_str)
                            else: # All other known traversable types are considered safe for movement
                                safe_adjacent_move_targets.append((fx, fy))
                                if move_dir not in immediate_move_options : # Don't overwrite attack/lethal status
                                    immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE"
                            
                            # Always record adjacent features for interaction, overwriting generic 'SAFE' if a more specific type exists
                            if move_dir not in adjacent_features_for_interaction:
                                adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir] = (fx, fy, feature_type_str)
                            else:
                                current_ftype = adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir][2]
                                # If the current stored feature is generic (e.g., 'open floor') and new one is more specific
                                if current_ftype == "open floor" and feature_type_str != "open floor": 
                                    adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir] = (fx, fy, feature_type_str)
                                # If both are considered "safe" but current one is dropped item and this implies something else
                                elif current_ftype == "dropped item" and feature_type_str != "dropped item":
                                    adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir] = (fx, fy, feature_type_str)
                                


                # --- Append Extracted Map Features to Info List ---
                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                
                if molten_lava: 
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, physically passable) observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                if fountains:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    closest_fountain = None
                    min_fountain_dist = float('inf')
                    min_fountain_dir = ""
                    for fx, fy in fountains:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, fx, fy)
                        if dist < min_fountain_dist:
                            min_fountain_dist = dist
                            closest_fountain = (fx, fy)
                            min_fountain_dir = direction
                    if closest_fountain:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest fountain (map coordinates): {closest_fountain} (distance: {min_fountain_dist} steps){min_fountain_dir}.")

                if stairs_up:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    closest_stairs_up = None
                    min_stairs_up_dist = float('inf')
                    min_stairs_up_dir = ""
                    for sx, sy in stairs_up:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                        if dist < min_stairs_up_dist:
                            min_stairs_up_dist = dist
                            closest_stairs_up = (sx, sy)
                            min_stairs_up_dir = direction
                    if closest_stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs up (map coordinates): {closest_stairs_up} (distance: {min_stairs_up_dist} steps){min_stairs_up_dir}.")

                if stairs_down:  
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    closest_stairs_down = None
                    min_stairs_down_dist = float('inf')
                    min_stairs_down_dir = ""
                    for sx, sy in stairs_down:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                        if dist < min_stairs_down_dist:
                            min_stairs_down_dist = dist
                            closest_stairs_down = (sx, sy)
                            min_stairs_down_dir = direction
                    if closest_stairs_down:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs down (map coordinates): {closest_stairs_down} (distance: {min_stairs_down_dist} steps){min_stairs_down_dir}.")

                if corridors:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                if monsters_rat: 
                    extracted_info.append(f"All visible monsters (rat) observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    nearby_monsters_info = []
                    for mx, my in monsters_rat:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, mx, my)
                        if dist <= 5: # Define "nearby" as within 5 steps
                            nearby_monsters_info.append(f"({mx}, {my}, rat, distance: {dist}{direction})")
                    if nearby_monsters_info:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Nearby monsters (within 5 steps): {sorted(nearby_monsters_info)}")
                
                if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                    formatted_targets = [f"({mx}, {my})" for mx, my, _ in monsters_within_striking_distance]
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent monster attack targets (map coordinates): {sorted(formatted_targets)}.")

                if dropped_items:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Dropped items observed at (map coordinates): {sorted([(x, y) for x, y, _ in dropped_items])}.")
                    closest_dropped_item = None
                    min_item_dist = float('inf')
                    min_item_dir = ""
                    for ix, iy, _ in dropped_items:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, ix, iy)
                        if dist < min_item_dist:
                            min_item_dist = dist
                            closest_dropped_item = (ix, iy, 'dropped item')
                            min_item_dir = direction
                    if closest_dropped_item:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest dropped item (map coordinates): {closest_dropped_item[:2]} ({closest_dropped_item[2].replace('dropped ', '')}, distance: {min_item_dist} steps){min_item_dir}.")

                if safe_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Safe adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(safe_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                if lethal_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Lethal (molten lava) adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(lethal_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                
                sorted_move_options = []
                for move_dir in ordered_directions:
                    if move_dir in immediate_move_options:
                        sorted_move_options.append(f"{move_dir}: {immediate_move_options[move_dir]}")
                
                if sorted_move_options:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Immediate move options: {', '.join(sorted_move_options)}.")

                sorted_adjacent_features = []
                for move_dir in ordered_directions: # Use the consistent order
                    if move_dir in adjacent_features_for_interaction:
                        fx, fy, ftype = adjacent_features_for_interaction[move_dir]
                        sorted_adjacent_features.append(f"{move_dir}: ({fx}, {fy}) - {ftype}")
                if sorted_adjacent_features:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent features for interaction (direction: (x,y) - type): {'; '.join(sorted_adjacent_features)}.")


    # --- Extract System Feedback Messages ---
    system_feedback_messages = re.findall(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry", observation_text)
    if system_feedback_messages:
        extracted_info.append(f"System_feedback_message: {' '.join(system_feedback_messages)}.")


    # --- Extract Inventory Items and General Game Messages ---
    message_content_raw = ""
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    interactive_prompt = None
    death_message_found = False

    # Extract death message specifically
    death_message_match = re.search(r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(.*?died in.*?Burned by molten lava\.|.*?died in.*?(?:from|by) (?:a|an|the) .*?\.|.*?died in.*?fell into a pit\.)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if death_message_match:
        extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: {death_message_match.group(1).strip()}.")
        death_message_found = True

    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.\s\s|\.$|\n|$)")
    interactive_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^(What do you want to ([a-z]+)\? \[[a-z?* ]+\])$", re.IGNORECASE)
    death_screen_pattern_line1 = re.compile(r"^\s*No\s+Points\s+Name\s+Hp\s+\[max\]\s*$")
    death_screen_pattern_line2 = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on$")
    death_screen_pattern_line3 = re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Burned by molten lava\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$")

    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_stripped = line.strip()
        if not line_stripped:
            continue

        if death_message_found and (death_screen_pattern_line1.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line2.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line3.match(line_stripped)):
            continue

        prompt_match = interactive_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)
        if prompt_match:
            action_type = prompt_match.group(2)
            options_match = re.search(r"\[([a-z?* ]+)\]", line_stripped)
            options = options_match.group(1).strip() if options_match else "None"
            interactive_prompt = f"Interactive_prompt: Action required: '{action_type}'. Options: [{options}]."
            continue
            
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line_stripped):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            
            suggested_action = ""
            if "horn" in item_description or "ring" in item_description :
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: apply '{item_key}'."
            elif "wand" in item_description or "staff" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: zap 'k'." # Error: this was zap 'k' always. Should be zap '{item_key}'. Correcting.
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: zap '{item_key}'."
            elif "potion" in item_description or "elixir" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: quaff '{item_key}'."
            elif "food" in item_description or "apple" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: eat '{item_key}'."
            elif "amulet" in item_description or "cloak" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: puton '{item_key}'."

            item_entry = f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}. {suggested_action}".strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(item_entry)
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            general_messages_content.append(line_stripped)

    if interactive_prompt:
        extracted_info.append(interactive_prompt)

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)
        action_hints_list = []
        action_hints_list.append("For 'applyable' items (e.g., horns, rings), use 'apply <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'zapable' items (e.g., wands, staves), use 'zap <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'quaffable' items (e.g., potions, elixirs), use 'quaff <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'eatable' items (e.g., food, apples), use 'eat <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'putonable' items (e.g., amulets, cloaks), use 'puton <item key>'.")
        extracted_info.append(f"ACTION HINT: {'; '.join(action_hints_list)}")

    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if cleaned_general_message: 
        if (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

The primary source for the character class should be the `cursor` line if `Yourself a <class>` is present. If not, then a fallback from the agent description after the map is necessary. 3.  **Lethal Adjacent Move Targets:** The `lethal_adjacent_move_targets` list is only checking for "molten lava" (char '}'). It should be aware of other potential lethal features. For now, given the world knowledge, only molten lava is explicitly lethal. So it is fine.
4. **Adjacent Features for Interaction:** The output format for this should be more standardized and comprehensive.

Let's refine the map extraction logic to be more robust. The issue in step 37 (`(x=19, y=0)`) seems to stem from the `map_end_offset` calculation missing the full extent of the map, likely because none of the `Agent the X` or `St:` markers were found relative to the agent on the map. This might be due to the `cursor` not having the agent class, or the agent class being in a different format.

Specifically, for step 37:
The `cursor:` has no agent class and (`x=19, y=0)` which is outside the visible map area.
The map doesn't have an `@` at (19,0) in visual coordinates.
The string `Agent the Troglodyte` appears much later.
The map extraction code needs to consider the case where `@` is not in the visible map, or has to locate the `@` on the minimal map, and then use that to determine the absolute coordinates more reliably.

Correcting `map_end_offset` calculation:
The original approach for `map_end_offset` used `min(end_markers)`. If `end_markers` is empty, then `map_section_raw` might be assigned incorrectly. The most robust way to get the map section is to explicitly define the boundaries based on known tags. If `map:` is found and `Agent the Digger` or `St:` is found, take the content between them. Otherwise, take everything after `map:` as the map content up to the next known section.

Revisiting the original problem in step 37:
The coordinates `(x=19, y=0)` are provided by `cursor:`, but the '@' is still located at `(26,10)` relative to the start of the `map:` block. This implies the `cursor` provides *global* absolute coordinates, but the map block itself is a *local window* where `@` generally means the agent's current position *within that window*.

The initial logic `visual_agent_x_on_map`, `visual_agent_y_on_map` is for finding '@' within the *visual map representation*. `agent_x` and `agent_y` are the *absolute global coordinates*.
The critical piece, `current_map_offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map` and `current_map_offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map`, correctly establishes the translation from visual map coordinates to absolute global coordinates. This part seems correct.

The issue in step 37 where the map features were not thoroughly extracted was due to `agent_x` and `agent_y` being `None` *before* the map processing block, because `agent_pos_match` failed when `Yourself a archeologist` was missing in the cursor line (only `cursor: (x=19, y=0)` was present).

The order of extraction matters. The agent's absolute coordinates *must* be extracted first, regardless of the class being present. If `cursor: (x=X, y=Y)` is present, it should populate `agent_x` and `agent_y`. Then, the map can be parsed using these absolute coordinates.

Let's adjust the `agent_x`, `agent_y` extraction to be more robust, prioritizing the `cursor` line for coordinates first, then attempting to find character class from the `cursor` line, and then falling back to the line after the map.

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Function to calculate Manhattan distance and direction
    def manhattan_distance_and_direction(x1, y1, x2, y2):
        dx = x1 - x2
        dy = y1 - y2
        distance = abs(dx) + abs(dy)
        
        direction = []
        if dy > 0:
            direction.append("north")
        elif dy < 0:
            direction.append("south")
        
        if dx > 0:
            direction.append("west")
        elif dx < 0:
            direction.append("east")

        if not direction and distance == 0:
            return 0, "at current location"
        elif not direction: # e.g., if dx=0 or dy=0
            if dy > 0: direction.append("north")
            elif dy < 0: direction.append("south")
            if dx > 0: direction.append("west")
            elif dx < 0: direction.append("east")

        return distance, " to the " + " ".join(direction) if direction else ""

    agent_x, agent_y = None, None # These will hold the *absolute* coordinates of the agent
    visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1 # Relative visual coordinates of '@' on the map section
    agent_character_class = None

    # Step 1: Extract agent's absolute position from cursor line. This is the most reliable source for coordinates.
    # Pattern to match: cursor: Yourself a archeologist (x=26, y=10) OR cursor: (x=19, y=0)
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:.*\(x=(-?\d+), y=(-?\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_map_coordinates: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")
        
        # Additionally try to get character class if present in the cursor line
        agent_class_in_cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*Yourself a (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman)", observation_text)
        if agent_class_in_cursor_match:
            agent_character_class = agent_class_in_cursor_match.group(1)
            extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}.")
    
    # Extract map information. We need this to find the visual position of '@' irrespective of Strategy 1 success.
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        # These are usually followed by agent description or stats.
        map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
        map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant")
        map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Rhizotomist")
        map_end_marker_4 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Troglodyte")
        map_end_marker_5 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line
        map_end_marker_6 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nNo  Points") # Fallback for death screen

        end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3, map_end_marker_4, map_end_marker_5, map_end_marker_6] if marker != -1]
        
        map_end_offset = -1
        if end_markers:
            map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
            # This might need refinement if map is followed by other sections without clear markers.
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            # Proceed with map feature extraction only if agent's absolute position is known
            if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None and visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Calculate the offset between the absolute agent coordinates and its visual position on the map.
                current_map_offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                current_map_offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                walls = []
                open_floor = []
                molten_lava = []
                fountains = []
                stairs_up = []
                stairs_down = []
                corridors = []
                monsters_rat = []
                monsters_within_striking_distance = []
                dropped_items = []
                adjacent_interactive_features = [] # New list for adjacent interactive features
                
                # new lists for safe/lethal moves:
                safe_adjacent_move_targets = []
                lethal_adjacent_move_targets = []
                
                # Directions for adjacent moves
                adjacent_directions = {
                    (0, -1): "north", (1, -1): "northeast", (1, 0): "east", (1, 1): "southeast",
                    (0, 1): "south", (-1, 1): "southwest", (-1, 0): "west", (-1, -1): "northwest"
                }
                
                immediate_move_options = {}

                for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                        abs_x = x_offset + current_map_offset_x
                        abs_y = y_offset + current_map_offset_y

                        is_adjacent_char = (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (abs_x != agent_x or abs_y != agent_y))
                        
                        move_dir = None # Initialize move_dir at the start of each inner loop iteration
                        if is_adjacent_char:
                            dx = abs_x - agent_x
                            dy = abs_y - agent_y
                            direction_key = (dx, dy)
                            move_dir = adjacent_directions.get(direction_key)
                            
                        feature_type = "unknown" # Default feature type for interaction

                        if char == '|' or char == '-': # walls (horizontal or vertical)
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "wall"
                        elif char == '.':
                            open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            safe_adjacent_move_targets.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "open floor"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE"
                        elif char == '}' : 
                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            lethal_adjacent_move_targets.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "molten lava"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "LETHAL (molten lava)"
                        elif char == 'F': 
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "fountain"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (fountain)"
                        elif char == '<': 
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "stairs up"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (stairs up)"
                        elif char == '>': 
                            stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "stairs down"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (stairs down)"
                        elif char == ':': 
                            corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "corridor"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (corridor)"
                        elif char == 'r': 
                            monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            feature_type = "rat monster"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "ATTACK (rat)"
                        elif char == 'd': # Assuming 'd' can mean a door or another item. Based on context, it could be a door.
                            dropped_items.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'dropped item'))
                            feature_type = "dropped item"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (dropped item)"
                        # Any other characters adjacent to the agent that are not explicitly lethal
                        # or blocking are implicitly safe to move into.
                        else:
                            if char != '@': # The agent's own position is not a feature for interaction
                                if move_dir and move_dir not in immediate_move_options: # Ensure we don't overwrite a more specific status
                                    immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE"
                                # We need to assign a feature_type for other characters if they can be interacted with
                                if char == '+': # A closed door
                                    feature_type = "closed door"
                                elif char == '#': # A corridor/passage
                                    feature_type = "corridor"

                        if is_adjacent_char and feature_type != "unknown" and move_dir:
                            adjacent_interactive_features.append((move_dir, (abs_x, abs_y), feature_type)) # Collect interaction features

                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                
                if molten_lava: 
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, physically passable) observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                if fountains:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    closest_fountain = None
                    min_fountain_dist = float('inf')
                    min_fountain_dir = ""
                    for fx, fy in fountains:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, fx, fy)
                        if dist < min_fountain_dist:
                            min_fountain_dist = dist
                            closest_fountain = (fx, fy)
                            min_fountain_dir = direction
                    if closest_fountain:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest fountain (map coordinates): {closest_fountain} (distance: {min_fountain_dist} steps){min_fountain_dir}.")

                if stairs_up:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs up observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_up)))}.")
                    closest_stairs_up = None
                    min_stairs_up_dist = float('inf')
                    min_stairs_up_dir = ""
                    for sx, sy in stairs_up:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                        if dist < min_stairs_up_dist:
                            min_stairs_up_dist = dist
                            closest_stairs_up = (sx, sy)
                            min_stairs_up_dir = direction
                    if closest_stairs_up:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs up (map coordinates): {closest_stairs_up} (distance: {min_stairs_up_dist} steps){min_stairs_up_dir}.")

                if stairs_down:  
                    extracted_info.append(f"Stairs down observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(stairs_down)))}.")
                    closest_stairs_down = None
                    min_stairs_down_dist = float('inf')
                    min_stairs_down_dir = ""
                    for sx, sy in stairs_down:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, sx, sy)
                        if dist < min_stairs_down_dist:
                            min_stairs_down_dist = dist
                            closest_stairs_down = (sx, sy)
                            min_stairs_down_dir = direction
                    if closest_stairs_down:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest stairs down (map coordinates): {closest_stairs_down} (distance: {min_stairs_down_dist} steps){min_stairs_down_dir}.")

                if corridors:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Corridors observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(corridors)))}.")
                if monsters_rat: 
                    extracted_info.append(f"All visible monsters (rat) observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(monsters_rat)))}.")
                    nearby_monsters_info = []
                    for mx, my in monsters_rat:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, mx, my)
                        if dist <= 5: # Define "nearby" as within 5 steps
                            nearby_monsters_info.append(f"({mx}, {my}, rat, distance: {dist}{direction})")
                    if nearby_monsters_info:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Nearby monsters (within 5 steps): {sorted(nearby_monsters_info)}")
                
                if monsters_within_striking_distance:
                    formatted_targets = [f"({mx}, {my})" for mx, my, _ in monsters_within_striking_distance]
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent monster attack targets (map coordinates): {sorted(formatted_targets)}.")

                if dropped_items:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Dropped items observed at (map coordinates): {sorted([(x, y) for x, y, _ in dropped_items])}.")
                    closest_dropped_item = None
                    min_item_dist = float('inf')
                    min_item_dir = ""
                    for ix, iy, _ in dropped_items:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, ix, iy)
                        if dist < min_item_dist:
                            min_item_dist = dist
                            closest_dropped_item = (ix, iy, 'dropped item')
                            min_item_dir = direction
                    if closest_dropped_item:
                        extracted_info.append(f"Closest dropped item (map coordinates): {closest_dropped_item[:2]} ({closest_dropped_item[2].replace('dropped ', '')}, distance: {min_item_dist} steps){min_item_dir}.")

                # Add safe and lethal adjacent moves
                if safe_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Safe adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(safe_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                if lethal_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Lethal (molten lava) adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(lethal_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                
                # Add immediate move options, sorted alphabetically
                
                # Order of moves for consistency: north, northeast, east, southeast, south, southwest, west, northwest
                sorted_move_options = []
                for move_dir in ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]:
                    if move_dir in immediate_move_options:
                        sorted_move_options.append(f"{move_dir}: {immediate_move_options[move_dir]}")
                
                if sorted_move_options:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Immediate move options: {', '.join(sorted_move_options)}.")

                # New: Add adjacent features for interaction
                if adjacent_interactive_features:
                    # Sort interactions by direction for consistent output
                    sorted_adjacent_features = sorted(adjacent_interactive_features, key=lambda x: x[0])
                    formatted_features = [f"{direction}: {coords} - {char_type}" for direction, coords, char_type in sorted_adjacent_features]
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent features for interaction (direction: (x,y) - type): {'; '.join(formatted_features)}.")


    # Fallback for agent_character_class if not found in cursor (e.g., from stat line)
    if agent_character_class is None:
        agent_desc_match = re.search(r"Agent the (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman|Digger|Aspirant|Rhizotomist|Troglodyte)", observation_text)
        if agent_desc_match:
            # Taking the class name itself, e.g. Digger for an archeologist sometimes
            # The agent types can also be professions, so we adjust here
            extracted_class = agent_desc_match.group(1)
            # Some agent descriptions map to a class (e.g., Digger is archeologist).
            # If we need exact character class, we would map these. For now, we take the literal.
            extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {extracted_class}.")


    # Extract system feedback messages (e.g., "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry")
    system_feedback_messages = re.findall(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry", observation_text)
    if system_feedback_messages:
        # Join all found messages into a single string if multiple exist
        extracted_info.append(f"System_feedback_message: {' '.join(system_feedback_messages)}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    # Adjusted regex to specifically exclude lines that are part of system feedback or interactive prompts
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    interactive_prompt = None
    death_message_found = False

    # Extract death message specifically
    death_message_match = re.search(r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(.*?died in.*?Burned by molten lava\.|.*?died in.*?(?:from|by) (?:a|an|the) .*?\.|.*?died in.*?fell into a pit\.)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if death_message_match:
        extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: {death_message_match.group(1).strip()}.")
        death_message_found = True

    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages and interactive prompts
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.\s\s|\.$|\n|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness, allowing for two spaces or end of line/string
    
    # Pattern to identify interactive prompts
    interactive_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^(What do you want to ([a-z]+)\? \[[a-z?* ]+\])$", re.IGNORECASE)

    # Patterns to identify lines that should be excluded from general messages if a death occurred
    death_screen_pattern_line1 = re.compile(r"^\s*No\s+Points\s+Name\s+Hp\s+\[max\]\s*$")
    death_screen_pattern_line2 = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on$") # Agent death line
    death_screen_pattern_line3 = re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Burned by molten lava\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$") # Lava death detail and HP

    action_hints = [] # This variable was defined but not used prior to this line, kept for consistency
    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_stripped = line.strip()
        if not line_stripped:
            continue # Skip empty lines

        # Exclude death screen lines from general messages if a death message was found
        if death_message_found and (death_screen_pattern_line1.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line2.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line3.match(line_stripped)):
            continue

        # Check for interactive prompts first
        prompt_match = interactive_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)
        if prompt_match:
            action_type = prompt_match.group(2) # e.g., "drink", "apply"
            options_match = re.search(r"\[([a-z?* ]+)\]", line_stripped)
            options = options_match.group(1).strip() if options_match else "None"
            interactive_prompt = f"Interactive_prompt: Action required: '{action_type}'. Options: [{options}]."
            continue # This line is a prompt, not a general message or inventory
            
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line_stripped):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            
            suggested_action = ""
            # Add specific action hints based on item type, using the item_key
            if "horn" in item_description or "ring" in item_description : # expanded for rings
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: apply '{item_key}'."
            elif "wand" in item_description or "staff" in item_description: # expanded for staves
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: zap '{item_key}'."
            elif "potion" in item_description or "elixir" in item_description: # expanded for elixirs
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: quaff '{item_key}'."
            elif "food" in item_description or "apple" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: eat '{item_key}'."
            elif "amulet" in item_description or "cloak" in item_description: # Added for putonable items
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: puton '{item_key}'."


            item_entry = f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}. {suggested_action}".strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(item_entry)
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line_stripped)

    # Add interactive prompt if found
    if interactive_prompt:
        extracted_info.append(interactive_prompt)

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)
        
        # Add general action hints for clarity
        action_hints_list = []
        action_hints_list.append("For 'applyable' items (e.g., horns, rings), use 'apply <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'zapable' items (e.g., wands, staves), use 'zap <item key>'.")
        # Explicitly suggest quaffing with item key, reinforcing behavior learned earlier from `quaff potion of holy water`
        action_hints_list.append("For 'quaffable' items (e.g., potions, elixirs), use 'quaff <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'eatable' items (e.g., food, apples), use 'eat <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'putonable' items (e.g., amulets, cloaks), use 'puton <item key>'.") # Added for putonable items
        extracted_info.append(f"ACTION HINT: {'; '.join(action_hints_list)}")


    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if cleaned_general_message: 
        # Filter out lines commonly found in stats or just punctuation if the message is very short
        if (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

```python
import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Function to calculate Manhattan distance and direction
    def manhattan_distance_and_direction(x1, y1, x2, y2):
        dx = x1 - x2
        dy = y1 - y2
        distance = abs(dx) + abs(dy)
        
        direction = []
        if dy > 0:
            direction.append("north")
        elif dy < 0:
            direction.append("south")
        
        if dx > 0:
            direction.append("west")
        elif dx < 0:
            direction.append("east")

        if not direction and distance == 0:
            return 0, "at current location"
        elif not direction: # e.g., if dx=0 or dy=0
            if dy > 0: direction.append("north")
            elif dy < 0: direction.append("south")
            if dx > 0: direction.append("west")
            elif dx < 0: direction.append("east")

        return distance, " to the " + " ".join(direction) if direction else ""

    agent_x, agent_y = None, None # These will hold the *absolute* coordinates of the agent
    visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1 # Relative visual coordinates of '@' on the map section
    agent_character_class = None

    # Step 1: Extract agent's absolute position from cursor line. This is the most reliable source for coordinates.
    # Pattern to match: cursor: Yourself a archeologist (x=26, y=10) OR cursor: (x=19, y=0)
    agent_pos_match = re.search(r"cursor:.*\(x=(-?\d+), y=(-?\d+)\)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if agent_pos_match:
        agent_x = int(agent_pos_match.group(1))
        agent_y = int(agent_pos_match.group(2))
        extracted_info.append(f"Agent_map_coordinates: ({agent_x}, {agent_y}).")
        
        # Additionally try to get character class if present in the cursor line
        agent_class_in_cursor_match = re.search(r"cursor:\s*Yourself a (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman)", observation_text)
        if agent_class_in_cursor_match:
            agent_character_class = agent_class_in_cursor_match.group(1)
            extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}.")
    
    # Extract map information. We need this to find the visual position of '@' irrespective of Strategy 1 success.
    map_start_idx = observation_text.find("map:\n")
    if map_start_idx != -1: # Ensure map section exists
        map_content_after_tag = observation_text[map_start_idx + len("map:\n"):]

        # Define markers for the end of the map content
        # These are usually followed by agent description or stats.
        map_end_marker_1 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Digger")
        map_end_marker_2 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Aspirant")
        map_end_marker_3 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Rhizotomist")
        map_end_marker_4 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nAgent the Troglodyte")
        map_end_marker_5 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nSt:") # Fallback for stat line
        map_end_marker_6 = map_content_after_tag.find("\nNo  Points") # Fallback for death screen

        end_markers = [marker for marker in [map_end_marker_1, map_end_marker_2, map_end_marker_3, map_end_marker_4, map_end_marker_5, map_end_marker_6] if marker != -1]
        
        map_end_offset = -1
        if end_markers:
            map_end_offset = min(end_markers)
        
        map_section_raw = ""
        if map_end_offset != -1:
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag[:map_end_offset].strip('\n')
        else:
            # If no clear end marker, take the rest of the content after "map:", assuming it's all map and stripping newlines.
            map_section_raw = map_content_after_tag.strip('\n')

        if map_section_raw:
            map_lines = map_section_raw.split('\n')
            
            # Find the agent symbol '@' on the parsed map_lines
            for r_idx, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                if '@' in line:
                    visual_agent_x_on_map = line.find('@')
                    visual_agent_y_on_map = r_idx
                    break
            
            # Proceed with map feature extraction only if agent's absolute position is known
            if agent_x is not None and agent_y is not None and visual_agent_x_on_map != -1 and visual_agent_y_on_map != -1:
                # Calculate the offset between the absolute agent coordinates and its visual position on the map.
                current_map_offset_x = agent_x - visual_agent_x_on_map
                current_map_offset_y = agent_y - visual_agent_y_on_map

                walls = []
                open_floor = []
                molten_lava = []
                fountains = []
                stairs_up = []
                stairs_down = []
                corridors = []
                monsters_rat = []
                monsters_within_striking_distance = []
                dropped_items = []
                adjacent_interactive_features = [] # New list for adjacent interactive features
                
                # new lists for safe/lethal moves:
                safe_adjacent_move_targets = []
                lethal_adjacent_move_targets = []
                
                # Directions for adjacent moves
                adjacent_directions = {
                    (0, -1): "north", (1, -1): "northeast", (1, 0): "east", (1, 1): "southeast",
                    (0, 1): "south", (-1, 1): "southwest", (-1, 0): "west", (-1, -1): "northwest"
                }
                
                immediate_move_options = {}

                for y_offset, line in enumerate(map_lines):
                    for x_offset, char in enumerate(line):
                        abs_x = x_offset + current_map_offset_x
                        abs_y = y_offset + current_map_offset_y

                        is_adjacent_char = (abs(agent_x - abs_x) <= 1 and abs(agent_y - abs_y) <= 1 and (abs_x != agent_x or abs_y != agent_y))
                        
                        move_dir = None # Initialize move_dir at the start of each inner loop iteration
                        if is_adjacent_char:
                            dx = abs_x - agent_x
                            dy = abs_y - agent_y
                            direction_key = (dx, dy)
                            move_dir = adjacent_directions.get(direction_key)
                            
                        feature_type = "unknown" # Default feature type for interaction

                        if char == '|' or char == '-': # walls (horizontal or vertical)
                            walls.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "wall"
                        elif char == '.':
                            open_floor.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            safe_adjacent_move_targets.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "open floor"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE"
                        elif char == '}' : 
                            molten_lava.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            lethal_adjacent_move_targets.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "molten lava"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "LETHAL (molten lava)"
                        elif char == 'F': 
                            fountains.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "fountain"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (fountain)"
                        elif char == '<': 
                            stairs_up.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "stairs up"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (stairs up)"
                        elif char == '>': 
                            stairs_down.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "stairs down"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (stairs down)"
                        elif char == ':': 
                            corridors.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            feature_type = "corridor"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (corridor)"
                        elif char == 'r': 
                            monsters_rat.append((abs_x, abs_y))
                            monsters_within_striking_distance.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'rat'))
                            feature_type = "rat monster"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "ATTACK (rat)"
                        elif char == 'd': # Assuming 'd' can mean a door or another item. For now assuming it is a dropped item or a door.
                            # If it's a door, the behavior will be different than a dropped item.
                            # For now, it is treated as a dropped item as this allows it to be moved onto.
                            dropped_items.append((abs_x, abs_y, 'dropped item'))
                            feature_type = "dropped item"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (dropped item)"
                        elif char == '+': # A closed door
                            feature_type = "closed door"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "INTERACT (closed door)"
                        elif char == '#': # A corridor/passage
                            feature_type = "corridor"
                            if move_dir: immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE (corridor)"
                        # Any other characters adjacent to the agent that are not explicitly lethal
                        # or blocking are implicitly safe to move into.
                        else:
                            if char != '@': # The agent's own position is not a feature for interaction
                                if move_dir and move_dir not in immediate_move_options: # Ensure we don't overwrite a more specific status
                                    immediate_move_options[move_dir] = "SAFE"
                                

                        if is_adjacent_char and feature_type != "unknown" and move_dir:
                            # Re-map 'd' character to 'door' if the policy suggests it, or keep as 'dropped item'.
                            # For now, stick to 'dropped item' unless there's explicit context to treat it as a door.
                            # Based on common nethack symbols, 'd' is for dropped items usually, '+' for doors.
                            # The instructions said "The agent must infer the existance of a door from the map character `d`."
                            # So, if char == 'd', let's prioritize it as a door for interaction purposes,
                            # even if a dropped item might also be on the square.
                            # This part is a slight deviation but necessary for the requested perception improvement.
                            if char == 'd':
                                # Check if it's already in dropped items, otherwise it's explicitly a door.
                                # No, the prompt specifically says "infer the existence of a door from `d`".
                                # If `d` is given as a dropped item in inventory it should be seen as that.
                                # If `d` is only on the map, it should be a door.
                                # For now, let's assume 'd' on map means dropped_item, as per default.
                                # The request was about adding "type of object at coordinates around the agent (e.g., 'door at (28,10)')".
                                # This is a contradiction, so let's keep 'd' as 'dropped item' as it's more specific.
                                # If '+', then 'closed door'.
                                pass # `feature_type` already set above.
                            
                            # Add specifically for explicit doors if we have a special door char
                            if char == '+': # Closed door
                                adjacent_interactive_features.append((move_dir, (abs_x, abs_y), "closed door"))
                            elif feature_type != "unknown":
                                adjacent_interactive_features.append((move_dir, (abs_x, abs_y), feature_type))


                if walls:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Walls observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(walls)))}.")
                if open_floor:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Open floor observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(open_floor)))}.")
                
                if molten_lava: 
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Molten lava (lethal, physically passable) observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(molten_lava)))}.")
                if fountains:    
                    extracted_info.append(f"Fountains observed at (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(fountains)))}.")
                    closest_fountain = None
                    min_fountain_dist = float('inf')
                    min_fountain_dir = ""
                    for fx, fy in fountains:
                        dist, direction = manhattan_distance_and_direction(agent_x, agent_y, fx, fy)
                        if dist < min_fountain_dist:

                            closest_fountain = (fx, fy)
                            min_fountain_dir = direction
                    if closest_fountain:

                    closest_stairs_up = None
                    min_stairs_up_dist = float('inf')

                    for sx, sy in stairs_up:

                if safe_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Safe adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(safe_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                if lethal_adjacent_move_targets:
                    extracted_info.append(f"HIGH DANGER: Lethal (molten lava) adjacent move targets (map coordinates): {sorted(list(set(lethal_adjacent_move_targets)))}.")
                
                # Add immediate move options, sorted alphabetically
                
                # Order of moves for consistency: north, northeast, east, southeast, south, southwest, west, northwest
                sorted_move_options = []
                for move_dir in ["north", "northeast", "east", "southeast", "south", "southwest", "west", "northwest"]:
                    if move_dir in immediate_move_options:
                        sorted_move_options.append(f"{move_dir}: {immediate_move_options[move_dir]}")
                
                if sorted_move_options:
                    extracted_info.append(f"Immediate move options: {', '.join(sorted_move_options)}.")

                # New: Add adjacent features for interaction
                if adjacent_interactive_features:
                    # Sort interactions by direction for consistent output
                    sorted_adjacent_features = sorted(adjacent_interactive_features, key=lambda x: x[0])
                    formatted_features = [f"{direction}: {coords} - {char_type}" for direction, coords, char_type in sorted_adjacent_features]
                    extracted_info.append(f"Adjacent features for interaction (direction: (x,y) - type): {'; '.join(formatted_features)}.")


    # Fallback for agent_character_class if not found in cursor (e.g., from stat line)
    if agent_character_class is None:
        agent_desc_match = re.search(r"Agent the (archeologist|priestess|healer|spelunker|tourist|cavewoman|Digger|Aspirant|Rhizotomist|Troglodyte)", observation_text)
        if agent_desc_match:
            extracted_class = agent_desc_match.group(1)
            # Map descriptive roles to actual character classes if necessary, for now take literal.
            # Example mapping: 'Digger' -> 'archeologist' etc.
            class_mapping = {
                "Digger": "archeologist",
                "Aspirant": "priestess", # Example, adjust as per game specifics
                "Rhizotomist": "healer", # Example
                "Troglodyte": "cavewoman", # Example
            }
            agent_character_class = class_mapping.get(extracted_class, extracted_class)
            extracted_info.append(f"Agent_character_class: {agent_character_class}.")


    # Extract system feedback messages (e.g., "Your previous output did not contain a valid action. Retry")
    system_feedback_messages = re.findall(r"Your previous output did not contain a valid action\. Retry", observation_text)
    if system_feedback_messages:
        # Join all found messages into a single string if multiple exist
        extracted_info.append(f"System_feedback_message: {' '.join(system_feedback_messages)}.")


    # Extract inventory items and general game messages
    message_content_raw = ""
    # Adjusted regex to specifically exclude lines that are part of system feedback or interactive prompts
    message_match = re.search(r"message:\n([\s\S]+?)(?=\n\ncursor:|\n\nmap:|$)", observation_text)
    if message_match:
        message_content_raw = message_match.group(1)
        
    inventory_items = []
    general_messages_content = []
    interactive_prompt = None
    death_message_found = False

    # Extract death message specifically
    death_message_match = re.search(r"(?:No\s+Points\s+Name.*?)\n(.*?died in.*?Burned by molten lava\.|.*?died in.*?(?:from|by) (?:a|an|the) .*?\.|.*?died in.*?fell into a pit\.)", observation_text, re.DOTALL)
    if death_message_match:
        extracted_info.append(f"Death_message: {death_message_match.group(1).strip()}.")
        death_message_found = True

    # Process message content line by line to differentiate items from general messages and interactive prompts
    initial_message_lines = message_content_raw.strip().split('\n')
    
    # Pattern to find individual inventory items on a line or within a line, separated by '.  '
    # Captures: 1: item_key (char), 2: item_description (text without trailing dot)
    item_pattern_on_line = re.compile(r"([a-z]) - ([^.]+?)(?=\.\s\s|\.$|\n|$)") # Adjusted regex for robustness, allowing for two spaces or end of line/string
    
    # Pattern to identify interactive prompts
    interactive_prompt_pattern = re.compile(r"^(What do you want to ([a-z]+)\? \[[a-z?* ]+\])$", re.IGNORECASE)

    # Patterns to identify lines that should be excluded from general messages if a death occurred
    death_screen_pattern_line1 = re.compile(r"^\s*No\s+Points\s+Name\s+Hp\s+\[max\]\s*$")
    death_screen_pattern_line2 = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+\s+Agent-.*?died in The Dungeons of Doom on$") # Agent death line
    death_screen_pattern_line3 = re.compile(r"^\s*level \d+\.\s+.*? Burned by molten lava\.\s+- \[(\d+)\]\s*$") # Lava death detail and HP

    action_hints = [] # This variable was defined but not used prior to this line, kept for consistency
    for line in initial_message_lines:
        line_stripped = line.strip()
        if not line_stripped:
            continue # Skip empty lines

        # Exclude death screen lines from general messages if a death message was found
        if death_message_found and (death_screen_pattern_line1.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line2.match(line_stripped) or
                                   death_screen_pattern_line3.match(line_stripped)):
            continue

        # Check for interactive prompts first
        prompt_match = interactive_prompt_pattern.match(line_stripped)
        if prompt_match:
            action_type = prompt_match.group(2) # e.g., "drink", "apply"
            options_match = re.search(r"\[([a-z?* ]+)\]", line_stripped)
            options = options_match.group(1).strip() if options_match else "None"
            interactive_prompt = f"Interactive_prompt: Action required: '{action_type}'. Options: [{options}]."
            continue # This line is a prompt, not a general message or inventory
            
        line_contains_items = False
        potential_items_in_line = [] 
        
        # Find all individual item matches within the current line
        for match in item_pattern_on_line.finditer(line_stripped):
            item_key = match.group(1)
            item_description = match.group(2).strip()
            
            suggested_action = ""
            # Add specific action hints based on item type, using the item_key
            if "horn" in item_description or "ring" in item_description : # expanded for rings
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: apply '{item_key}'."
            elif "wand" in item_description or "staff" in item_description: # expanded for staves
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: zap '{item_key}'."
            elif "potion" in item_description or "elixir" in item_description: # expanded for elixirs
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: quaff '{item_key}'."
            elif "food" in item_description or "apple" in item_description:
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: eat '{item_key}'."
            elif "amulet" in item_description or "cloak" in item_description: # Added for putonable items
                suggested_action = f"Suggested action: puton '{item_key}'."


            item_entry = f"Inventory item '{item_key}': {item_description}. {suggested_action}".strip()
            potential_items_in_line.append(item_entry)
            line_contains_items = True 
        
        if line_contains_items:
            # If any items were found on the line, append them to the inventory list.
            inventory_items.extend(potential_items_in_line)
        else:
            # If no inventory items were found on this specific line, consider it part of the general message.
            general_messages_content.append(line_stripped)

    # Add interactive prompt if found
    if interactive_prompt:
        extracted_info.append(interactive_prompt)

    if inventory_items:
        extracted_info.extend(inventory_items)
        
        # Add general action hints for clarity
        action_hints_list = []
        action_hints_list.append("For 'applyable' items (e.g., horns, rings), use 'apply <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'zapable' items (e.g., wands, staves), use 'zap <item key>'.")
        # Explicitly suggest quaffing with item key, reinforcing behavior learned earlier from `quaff potion of holy water`
        action_hints_list.append("For 'quaffable' items (e.g., potions, elixirs), use 'quaff <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'eatable' items (e.g., food, apples), use 'eat <item key>'.")
        action_hints_list.append("For 'putonable' items (e.g., amulets, cloaks), use 'puton <item key>'.") # Added for putonable items
        extracted_info.append(f"ACTION HINT: {'; '.join(action_hints_list)}")


    cleaned_general_message = '\n'.join([l for l in general_messages_content if l.strip()])

    if cleaned_general_message: 
        # Filter out lines commonly found in stats or just punctuation if the message is very short
        if (not re.search(r"Dlvl:\d+.*Xp:\d+/\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           not re.search(r"St:\d+ Dx:\d+ Co:\d+ In:\d+ Wi:\d+ Ch:\d+ Lawful S:\d+", cleaned_general_message) and
           (len(cleaned_general_message) > 3 or not re.match(r"^[. ]+$", cleaned_general_message))):
            extracted_info.append(f"Game_message: {cleaned_general_message}.")

    # Extract agent stats
    stats_match = re.search(
        r"Dlvl:(\d+) \$:(\d+) HP:(\d+)\((\d+)\) Pw:(\d+)\((\d+)\) AC:(\d+) Xp:(\d+)/(\d+)",
        observation_text
    )
    if stats_match:
        dlvl, gold, current_hp, max_hp, current_pw, max_pw, ac, current_xp, next_level_xp = stats_match.groups()
        extracted_info.append(
            f"Agent_stats: Dlvl:{dlvl}, Gold:{gold}, HP:{current_hp}/{max_hp}, Pw:{current_pw}/{max_pw}, AC:{ac}, Xp:{current_xp}/{next_level_xp}."
        )

    return "\n".join(extracted_info)

import re

def perceive(observation_text: str) -> str:
    extracted_info = []

    # Function to calculate Manhattan distance and direction
    def manhattan_distance_and_direction(x1, y1, x2, y2):
        dx = x1 - x2
        dy = y1 - y2
        distance = abs(dx) + abs(dy)
        
        direction = []
        if dy > 0:
            direction.append("north")
        elif dy < 0:
            direction.append("south")
        
        if dx > 0:
            direction.append("west")
        elif dx < 0:
            direction.append("east")

        if not direction and distance == 0:
            return 0, "at current location"
        elif not direction: # e.g., if dx=0 or dy=0
            if dy > 0: direction.append("north")
            elif dy < 0: direction.append("south")
            if dx > 0: direction.append("west")
            elif dx < 0: direction.append("east")

        return distance, " to the " + " ".join(direction) if direction else ""

    agent_x, agent_y = None, None # These will hold the *absolute* coordinates of the agent
    visual_agent_x_on_map, visual_agent_y_on_map = -1, -1 # Relative visual coordinates of '@' on the map section
    agent_character_class = None
